candidates.why: Clearly labeled top-line revenue and matches the Total Revenue definition; other candidates are finance or tax related, not operating revenue.
Finance-related income, not operating revenue; only used as a fallback if no proper revenue line existed, which is not the case here.
Interest income, not operating revenue; inappropriate as revenue proxy while a clear Total Revenue line exists.
Net finance result, not operating revenue; not suitable while a clear Total Revenue line exists.
Tax line, clearly not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Net income measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the year
candidates.why: Comprehensive income, not revenue.
Special items, not top-line revenue.
Broad operating expense aggregate that appears to include both COGS and SG&A; we prefer the more specific direct cost line when available.
Describes direct input costs, matching the notion of cost of goods sold / cost of sales for this business.
Mixed other operating items, not clearly direct production costs.
Transaction-related overhead, not direct cost of sales.
Overhead (SG&A-type), not direct production costs.
Overhead (SG&A-type), not direct production costs.
Likely overhead services (consulting, etc.), not clearly direct COGS.
SG&A component, not COGS.
Result metric (net), not an expense component.

candidates.row_label: Net actuarial gain/loss on pension expense
candidates.why: Actuarial remeasurement, not COGS.
Special items, not structural COGS.
Inventory component, balance sheet, not period COGS flow.
Liability, not the cost of sales flow itself.
Explicit SG&A label; values appear blank in recent preview but likely populated historically. Should be included as an SG&A component per rules.
Large aggregate that seems to include both COGS and SG&A; rules advise avoiding such totals when better components exist.
Classic SG&A-type overhead (personnel) separate from direct materials costs; strong SG&A component candidate.
Labeled as SG&A other; clearly an SG&A component line.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. Selected multiple overhead components rather than the broad 'Operating expenses' total, to avoid mixing COGS and SG&A. Went beyond the provided candidate list to include additional clear SG&A-type lines: 'Rent and other office expenses', 'Travel expenses', 'External services', and 'M&A expenses'. These appear to be operating overhead rather than direct cost of sales. 'Selling, General & Admin' is included even though recent values look blank, per rule (b) to capture earlier years. Please review that these component choices align with the company’s classification of SG&A vs direct costs. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Rent and other office expenses, [Income Statement] Travel expenses, [Income Statement] External services, [Income Statement] M&A expenses

candidates.row_label: Depreciation of Financial Lease Right-of-Use Assets in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Only line explicitly tied to research & development; although it is a depreciation component, it is clearly part of R&D expense and is the closest available R&D-related item.

final_choice.row_label: Depreciation of Financial Lease Right-of-Use Assets in Research & Development Expenses

variables.notes: No explicit total R&D expense line exists; using the only R&D-specific line available (a depreciation component within R&D).

candidates.why: Clearly labeled interest expense line within finance expenses; primary choice for interest expense series.

Represents allocation of profit, not interest; not suitable for XINT.

Balance sheet liability, not an expense flow; not suitable for XINT.

variables.notes: Provided a priority list for interest expense: primary is the consolidated 'Interest Expenses' line; lease-related interest is secondary if the primary line is missing in some years.

candidates.why: Component of equity but not total shareholders’ equity; less appropriate than a clearly labeled total equity attributable to owners.
Not clearly standard equity; likely technical or unused; not suitable as main BE measure.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the parent
candidates.why: Best match for book equity attributable to common shareholders, excluding non-controlling interests, which aligns with BE definition.
Includes non-controlling interests; acceptable fallback if owner-equity line were unavailable, but here we have a better line.
Technical balancing line, not preferred when a proper total equity line is present.
Technical balancing line, not preferred when a proper total equity line is present.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing line, not equity itself.
Represents total assets/liabilities+equity, not equity alone.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the parent

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit; useful for flows, but for balance-sheet minority interest we prefer the equity statement on the Balance Sheet sheet.
Duplicate concept on income statement; again, a flow allocation rather than stock; balance sheet lines are preferred for BE+MIB denominator.
Balance sheet line for equity attributable to non-controlling interests; primary choice for minority interest (MIB).
Alternative label for same concept on the balance sheet; included as secondary fallback in case of label changes across years.

variables.notes: Used balance sheet minority interest lines (stock) rather than income statement allocations (flows), per BE+MIB denominator convention.
